req.title: Open checking account (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-076 defines three distinct concern areas: (1) valid deposit range [25, 50000] accepting any amount within, (2) boundary values at 25 and 50000 and their immediate neighbours 24/50001, (3) idempotency semantics for duplicate submissions, and (4) authorisation guard for unverified customers. EP covers the happy-path valid partition and the unverified-customer partition. BVA targets the exact lower/upper bounds and their neighbours. STC models the state transition from first-submission -> account-created -> duplicate-submission -> same-result-returned. NEG covers below-minimum deposit and unverified customer rejections. DT captures the multi-condition matrix (verified x deposit-range). Together these seven tests satisfy the minimum three required while achieving broad requirement coverage traceable to every clause in R-FIN-076.

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_valid_deposit_verified_customer
preconditions: unique Idempotency-Key generated for this test run
actions: POST /accounts/checking with verified customer token, deposit=1000, unique Idempotency-Key
expected: welcome-notification dispatched (notification_sent == true or equivalent signal)
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_checking_account_valid_deposit_verified_customer(
    verified_customer_token, api_client
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000}
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }

    resp = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("account_type") == "checking"
    assert body.get("notification_sent") is True

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission
preconditions: single fixed Idempotency-Key shared across both requests in this test
first submission succeeds and creates an account
actions: POST /accounts/checking (second time) with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key
expected: Second call returns same HTTP status (201) and identical account_id
No duplicate account created; system remains in account-created state
trace: R-FIN-076: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_checking_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission(
    verified_customer_token, api_client
):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 500}
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
    }

    resp1 = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    body1 = resp1.json()
    account_id_first = body1["account_id"]

    resp2 = api_client.post("/accounts/checking", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 201
    body2 = resp2.json()
    assert body2["account_id"] == account_id_first

tests.name: test_open_checking_account_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: initial_deposit=1000 (valid range, isolating the verification condition)
unique Idempotency-Key generated for this test run
actions: POST /accounts/checking with unverified customer token, deposit=1000, unique Idempotency-Key
expected: response body contains error indicating customer not verified
